tate of Califorllia 
Fair Political Practices Commissio1l. 
P.o. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
William R. Galstan 
City Attorney 
City of Antioch 
P.O. Box 130 
Antioch, CA 
94509 
Dear Mr. Galstan:

(916) 322·5662

enror •• m ..... 
322-<>.Ui 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-84-297 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Antioch 
City Mayor Joel Keller concerning his obligations under the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
The following advice is provided pursuant to Government Code 
Section 831l4(b) .11 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
May Mr. Keller participate in decisions concerning the sale 
of land by the City of Antioch to Contra Costa County and the 
rezoning of that land? 
CONCLUSION 
Mr. Keller may parcicipate in decisions concerning the sale 
of land by the City of Antioch to Contra Costa County and the 
rezoning of that land. 
FACTS 
In your letter you presented the following facts: 
Mr. Keller is employed as a Probation Officer for 
Contra Costa County. 
The City of Antioch has a piece 
of vacant surplus land which it proposes co sell to 
1,1 
Government Code Sections 81000-91014. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. 


William R. Galstan 
December 13, 1984 
Page 2 
Contra Costa County. 
The County plans to erect and 
maintain an office building, which would house social 
services, probation department, and other County 
functions. 
In order for this to happen, the City 
Council will have to approve a rezoning from single 
family residential to commercial, and the Council will 
have to approve an agreement for the sale of the 
property. 
Mr. Keller is currently assigned to the Probation 
Department office in Concord. 
He occasionally has to 
visit the present Anitoch office, which would be 
relocated to the new building. 
Like any Probation 
Officer, Mr. Keller may in the future be transferred, 
which could include a transfer to the new County 
facility described above. 
DISCUSSION 
Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, 
participating in the making, or in any way attempting to use his 
official position to influence a governmental decision in which 
he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest. 
An 
official has a financial interest in a decision if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial-effect on: 
(c) 
Any source of income • • • aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($2S0) or more in value provided 
to, received by or promised to the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is 
made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management •••• 
Section 87103. 
Mr. Keller receives a salary from Contra Costa County. 
However, for purposes of the Political Reform Act, salary 
received from a governmental entity is exempted from the 
def ini tion of 
If income. If 
(Section 82030.) 
Therefore, the salary 
paid to Mr. Keller as a Contra Costa County probation officer 
does not constitute "income" and the County is not a source of 
income to Mr. Keller. 


William R. Galstan 
December 13, 1984 
Page 3 
Mr. Keller is also an employee of Contra Costa County. 
However, subdivision (d) of Section 87103 applies only if the 
public official is an employee of, or holds certain positions 
in, a business entity. 
Section 82005 specifies that "business 
entity" means an organization or enterprise operated for 
profit. 
Thus, Mr. Keller is not an employee of a "business 
entity" for purposes of the Political Reform Act. 
Therefore, Mr. Keller's salary and employment as a probation 
officer for Contra Costa County do not provide a basis for his 
disqualification on decisions concerning the sale of land by the 
City of Antioch to the County and the rezoning of that land. 
If you have any further questions, please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
1{ttt~ t .~t))1t-r1~ 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


HNlTOCH 
j 
~, , 
f/ __ :-'.' 
November 29, 1984 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento CA 
95804 
Re: 
Request for Opinion 
Dear Sir or Madam: 
Antioch Mayor Joel Keller has asked me to request 
your opinion on the following issues. 
Mr. Keller is employed as a Probation Officer for 
Contra Costa County. 
The City of Antioch has a piece of 
vacant surplus land which it proposes to sell to Contra Costa 
County. 
The County plans to erect and maintain an office 
building, which would house social services, probation 
department, and other County functions. 
In order for this to 
happen, the City Council will have to approve a rezoning from 
single family residential to commercial, and the Council will 
have to approve an agreement for the sale of the property. 
Mr. Keller is currently assigned to the Probation 
Department office in Concord. 
He occasionally has to visit 
the present Antioch office, which would be relocated to the 
new building. 
Like any Probation Officer, Mr. Keller may in 
the future be transferred, which could include a transfer to 
the new County facility described above. 
Please advise whether in your opinion Mr. Keller 
would have any conflict of interest in voting on either the 
rezoning or the agreement for transferring the property to the 
County. 
I would note that the City has no jurisdiction over 
design approval of the new structure. 
Thank you for your 
assistance. 
WRG/kw 
Very truly yours, 
WILLIAM R. GALSTAN 
City Attorney
